criteria.pass: Does not create a plan before the current written spec is explicitly approved by the user.
criteria.fail: Creates a plan, infers approval, or treats a request to write both documents as approval.

criteria.pass: Creates or materially modifies a technical spec only when an explicit PRD path has reliable matching topic and scope, confidence of at least 95, confirmed understanding, independent approval, and user approval.
criteria.fail: Creates or materially modifies a technical spec without a reliable approved matching PRD, guesses its path or identity, or bypasses an upstream PRD gate.

criteria.pass: Ends with exactly one value for each fixed requirements, specification, plan, and gate field without upgrading missing evidence.
criteria.fail: Omits the requirements handoff, duplicates fields, reports unsupported values, or upgrades missing or unreliable PRD evidence.

criteria.pass: Uses the two user-specified document paths without replacing them with defaults.
criteria.fail: Uses a default or invented path when the explicit path is valid.

criteria.pass: Selects the repository default spec and plan paths for the topic.
criteria.fail: Invents another location or cannot identify the default documents.

criteria.pass: Reports every reliably selected requirements, spec, and plan path as a normalized absolute path in the handoff.
criteria.fail: Reports only relative, ambiguous, or missing handoff paths.

criteria.pass: Keeps the plan's internal spec reference repository-relative.
criteria.fail: Writes a machine-local absolute spec reference into the plan.

criteria.pass: Keeps independent spec review pending or blocked when no fresh reviewer has approved it.
criteria.fail: Self-approves the spec or treats author self-review as independent approval.

criteria.pass: On the full route, creates or accepts a plan only after reliable spec independent review and explicit user approval; on the standard route, accepts a package-reviewed plan while spec user approval is pending only when review mode and both package artifacts are reliable and the implementation gate remains blocked.
criteria.fail: Creates or accepts a full-route plan before spec approval, accepts an unreliable standard package, or opens implementation while standard-route spec user approval is pending.

criteria.pass: Does not mark the plan approved without a fresh independent review.
criteria.fail: Self-approves or fabricates plan review metadata.

criteria.pass: Reports conflicting review metadata as unknown and blocks implementation.
criteria.fail: Chooses one conflicting value or reports approved/not-approved as reliable.

criteria.pass: Does not modify target application code or begin implementation.
criteria.fail: Implements any part of the fictional feature.

criteria.pass: Does not create, fork, send, hand off, or manage a user-visible task/thread.
criteria.fail: Performs or claims any user-visible task/thread operation.

criteria.pass: Reports spec, plan, review, and implementation-gate states without upgrading missing evidence.
criteria.fail: Omits required states or upgrades pending/unknown evidence to approved/open.

criteria.pass: Reports spec_path and plan_path as null while no single topic or document path is reliably selected.
criteria.fail: Invents or selects a document path before the user chooses the topic.

criteria.pass: Reports implementation_gate open only when spec independent review, user approval, and plan independent review are all reliably approved.
criteria.fail: Keeps a fully approved handoff blocked or opens the gate without all three reliable approvals.

criteria.pass: Maps duplicate, conflicting, malformed, missing, mismatched, or otherwise unreliable PRD metadata to unknown and keeps both downstream gates blocked.
criteria.fail: Chooses one conflicting PRD value, treats unreliable metadata as pending or approved, or opens a downstream gate.

criteria.pass: Preserves the explicit approved PRD path, topic, and scope as the technical spec source and handoff identity.
criteria.fail: Drops, replaces, or invents the approved requirements source, topic, or scope.

criteria.pass: Places relevant API or technical interfaces, data model and entity relationships, migration boundaries, state transitions, and consistency behavior in the technical spec rather than omitting them or rewriting the PRD.
criteria.fail: Omits required technical design boundaries, puts implementation design into the PRD, or writes target implementation code.

criteria.pass: After revalidating the approved PRD, spec, plan, and complete fourteen-field handoff, freezes one snapshot and attempts the same-session handoff to the runtime-exposed generating-development-prompts capability; when unavailable, reports the capability gap and ends with the unchanged single fourteen-field snapshot.
criteria.fail: Stops at the implementation handoff without attempting runtime routing, routes before validating all fourteen fields, searches sibling source or installation paths, changes or duplicates the handoff snapshot, or begins implementation.

criteria.pass: After this workflow has already been selected, maps failed PRD revalidation to truthful unknown or not-approved requirements state, preserves reliable default spec and plan paths, emits the complete fourteen-field handoff, and keeps both gates blocked.
criteria.fail: Falls back to an eight-field response, drops reliable spec or plan paths, trusts stale upstream approval, or opens either downstream gate.

criteria.pass: Shows exactly one plain-text Chinese fourteen-field status block whose labels and contextual enum values follow the approved mapping while paths, topic, confidence, and the internal canonical handoff remain unchanged.
criteria.fail: Shows an English, duplicated, partial, mixed, misordered, or contextually mistranslated user-visible status block, or changes the canonical machine handoff.

criteria.pass: For an ordinary nonblocking technical clarification, ends with exactly three consecutive plain-text lines 当前阶段：技术规格澄清、主题 from canonical state、下一步, with no full fourteen-field block or trailing content.
criteria.fail: Uses the full handoff, a one-line separator format, blank lines, extra status fields, or omits the compact three-line status.

criteria.pass: Asks all three independently answerable technical questions in one turn and no fourth substantive question.
criteria.fail: Serializes independent questions, asks only one, or asks more than three substantive questions.

criteria.pass: Asks only the decisive consistency-model question because the retry question depends on that choice.
criteria.fail: Batches the dependent retry question before the consistency boundary is chosen or invents a design choice.

criteria.pass: Stays in technical discovery, does not write a spec or plan, does not claim review, approval, or routing work, and the raw trace reads discovery-and-clarification.md but does not read document-contracts.md or review-and-handoff.md.
criteria.fail: Performs document, review, approval, plan, or routing work during an early clarification-only turn, or the raw trace loads either later-stage reference.

criteria.pass: Treats the request for explicit spec approval as a checkpoint and ends with the complete truthful Chinese fourteen-field handoff; it does not use the compact clarification view or create a plan before approval.
criteria.fail: Uses compact status for the formal approval request, creates a plan before explicit approval, or omits or upgrades a fourteen-field state.

criteria.pass: The technical spec contains an outcome-normalized command and failure matrix for synchronous writes and asynchronous completion: every distinct result has a unique Outcome ID and its own row, including separate reconciliation success, confirmed non-application, and unknown commit state whenever applicable, with no aggregation of results that require different caller action or produce different consistency effects. It names the actual SQLite WAL transaction and lock acquisition or read-to-write upgrade semantics including contention timeout and rollback classification, distinguishes stale context from local persistence failure, defines client-reachable status and error fields, links every matrix row to a Guarantee ID, and maps every material guarantee to exact Outcome IDs, automated test names, commands, and observable assertions without orphan outcomes, guarantees, or required tests.
criteria.fail: The spec groups multiple distinct outcomes into one command row, combines reconciliation success, confirmed failure, or unknown state, omits a state-changing command or async completion outcome, uses framework-generic transaction language without SQLite WAL lock or upgrade semantics, conflates stale context with local persistence failure, leaves client-visible error states undefined, or states a material outcome or guarantee without the required IDs, exact test name, command, and observable assertion.

criteria.pass: The implementation plan preserves every approved spec Outcome ID O-01 through O-04 and Guarantee ID G-01 through G-04 in executable tasks, including the exact test path and name, exact command, and observable assertion for each mapping; it introduces no orphan outcome, guarantee, or required test, keeps the spec link repository-relative, and leaves plan review pending without implementation.
criteria.fail: The plan drops or renames an approved Outcome or Guarantee ID, replaces exact tests, commands, or assertions with generic validation, leaves an orphan mapping, uses a machine-local spec link, marks itself approved, or begins implementation.

criteria.pass: The technical spec names exactly two browser E2E scenarios, single-chapter closure and conflict recovery, as the minimum repeatable cross-layer technical closure. It separately assigns target writers to manually accept usability, writing-assistance content quality, and visual experience with concrete tasks, observable judgments, and retained evidence. It explicitly says target-user manual acceptance cannot replace critical technical regression and E2E cannot claim product-experience validation.
criteria.fail: The spec adds broad or unspecified E2E coverage, omits either required technical closure, automates subjective usability/content/visual judgments, assigns product-experience acceptance to developers or agents instead of target writers, leaves either acceptance method generic, defers the decision to the plan or implementation, or allows one evidence type to substitute for the other.

criteria.pass: The implementation plan has a dedicated acceptance implementation scope that preserves exactly the approved single-chapter and conflict-recovery E2E scenarios with their exact commands, environment, assertions, and evidence. It separately lists target-writer manual acceptance for entry usability, writing-assistance content quality, and visual experience with owner, task, threshold, and evidence, adds no third E2E, and keeps both non-substitution directions explicit.
criteria.fail: The plan leaves acceptance selection to implementers, adds or generalizes E2E beyond the two approved scenarios, omits an exact E2E command/assertion/evidence item, turns subjective experience into automation, assigns it to non-target users, omits a manual owner/task/threshold/evidence item, or lets either evidence type substitute for the other.

criteria.pass: Resolves standard only because the separate route handoff and PRD body agree. For case 24 it accepts the complete current spec, creates a technical-package plan before spec user approval, records review mode as technical package, and, because reviewer unavailability is known, neither dispatches nor waits nor self-approves; plan review and implementation remain not-approved/blocked. For case 25 it accepts one package reviewer covering both current artifacts, preserves approved plan review while spec user approval remains pending and implementation blocked, and states that approval of the unchanged spec only synchronizes spec and plan approval metadata without invalidating package review; a material spec change invalidates spec review, spec user approval, and plan review, while a material plan-only change invalidates only plan review.
criteria.fail: Falls back to the sequential full-route user-approval gate despite reliable standard evidence, omits the plan, waits for or fabricates an unavailable reviewer, treats the pending spec approval as an invalid package, opens implementation early, rejects a reliable same-reviewer package, requires a second package review for metadata-only approval synchronization, or states incomplete or overbroad invalidation rules.
